div.h1: Kernel File Descriptor Table: Listening FD vs. Client FD

g.text: FD Leak: What Happens When close(client_fd) Is Skipped
Step 1 | fd[N] stays allocated | FD slot never freed
Step 2 | sk_refcnt stays at 1 | kernel socket not destroyed
Step 3 | TCP FIN never sent | peer waits in CLOSE_WAIT
Step 4 | recv buf (87KB) not freed | kernel memory slowly consumed
Step 6 | EMFILE: no new accepts | server effectively dead
Detect | ls /proc/PID/fd | wc -l | count rises, never falls
Fix: close(client_fd) on EVERY exit path in handle_client()
reserved by proc | fd[0] fd[1] fd[2] | stdin/stdout/stderr
Each client_fd = 1 slot; leak 1020 → server dead
fd[1023]| file* | (last slot — EMFILE if all full)
NOTE: never recv() or send() on this socket
sk_refcnt | atomic_t | 1 ← LEAK if close() skipped
read/write via recv()/send() — MUST close() after use
